session_hint: the Kafka consumer group lag spike investigation
question: What was the root cause of the Kafka consumer group lag spiking on the payments topic?
turns: user
Consumer group lag on our payments topic jumped from ~500 to over 2 million messages over a 3-hour window. No schema changes or deployments.
user
Checked broker disk I/O across all three brokers: all under 40% utilization, no throttling events in broker logs. Disk is fine.
user
Looked at inter-broker replication network — no packet loss, replication lag is under 10ms. Network between brokers is healthy.
user
Reviewed partition count: payments topic has 24 partitions, we have 8 consumer pods. Ratio is fine, no partition starvation.
user
Pulled per-consumer metrics. Seven consumers processing 800-900 messages/sec each. One consumer pod stuck at 3 messages/sec. Huge outlier.
user
Checked GC logs on the slow pod: no GC pauses over 100ms. Not a garbage collection issue.
user
Traced the slow consumer in detail: it holds partition locks while making a synchronous blocking HTTP call to the fraud-check service. That service has been timing out intermittently at 30 seconds.
user

The slow consumer periodically misses its max.poll.interval.ms heartbeat window. The group coordinator interprets this as a dead consumer and triggers a rebalance. During rebalance ALL consumers pause for 30-60 seconds while partitions are reassigned.
user
So the lag isn't just from one slow consumer — it's from repeated group rebalances triggered by that consumer, which stall all 8 consumers simultaneously. That's why lag grew so fast.
user

Root cause confirmed: a single slow consumer holding partition locks through long synchronous HTTP calls triggers repeated group rebalances, halting the entire consumer group during each reassignment cycle.
user
Fix: moved the fraud-check HTTP call outside the poll loop using an async client with a short timeout. Lag dropping now at 15k messages/sec.
user
Broker disk, network replication, and partition count were all clean. Adding a per-consumer lag alert and a max.poll.interval.ms alert. Incident closed.
